2026-04-16T03:13:34.5081759Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T03:13:34.5082196Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T03:13:34.5082568Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T03:13:34.5082895Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T03:13:34.5083134Z [36;1m[0m

Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. The migration added `ConversationThreadClientSettings`, new `CollectionResults` for conversation pagination, and updated `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`. The package has offered Conversation APIs since `1.3.0-beta.1`, and these are now fully present in the public API surface (309 additions to the API listing files), but they remain entirely absent from the README.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` are not mentioned anywhere in `README.md` — the README only documents `NotificationMessagesClient` and `MessageTemplateClient`\\n- The **Key concepts** section lists only `NotificationMessagesClient`; `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`, and `ConversationThreadClientSettings` are not described\\n- No **Examples** section exists for Conversation APIs (create/get/delete conversations, send/list messages, add/remove participants, read receipts, typing indicators)\\n- Auto-generated sample files (`Samples_ConversationAdministrationClient.cs`, `Samples_ConversationThreadClient.cs`) exist in `tests/Samples/` but contain only compilation-only `[Ignore]` tests with no `#region Snippet:` regions — no human-readable snippets are wired into the README\\n- The CHANGELOG `1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section is completely empty despite the generator migration introducing `ConversationThreadClientSettings` and `CollectionResults` pagination types\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section to document the migration changes:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n- Added `ConversationThreadClientSettings` to support configuration-based setup of `ConversationThreadClient` via `IConfiguration`.\\n- Added strongly-typed collection result types (`ConversationAdministrationClientGetConversationsCollectionResult` and async variants) for paginating conversation lists.\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n- Migrated to the new TypeSpec-based code generator. Generated code is now located under `Generated/` at the package root.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md`\\n\\nEdit the README to add the Conversation API key concepts and examples. Changes needed:\\n\\n1. **Key concepts section** — replace or extend the current sparse paragraph:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Key concepts\\n\\n- `NotificationMessagesClient` provides the functionality to send notification messages (WhatsApp text, template, and media messages).\\n- `MessageTemplateClient` provides the functionality to list available WhatsApp message templates.\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` manages conversation lifecycle: create, retrieve, list, and delete conversations.\\n- `ConversationThreadClient` interacts with conversation threads: send messages, list messages, manage participants, send read receipts, and send typing indicators.\\n- `ConversationThreadClientSettings` enables DI/configuration-based setup of `ConversationThreadClient` when using `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration`.\\n```\\n\\n2. **Authenticate the client** subsection — add a snippet placeholder for `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` authentication:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Conversation clients\\n\\n`ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` support both connection string and token credential authentication.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n3. **Examples section** — add a new subsection for Conversation APIs after the existing notification examples:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Work with conversations\\n\\n#### Create a conversation\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### List conversations\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### Send a message in a conversation\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### List messages in a conversation\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### Add a participant to a conversation\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new human-readable sample file adminClient.DeleteConversationAsync(createResponse.Value.Id);\\n#endif\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task ListConversationsAsync()\\n        {\\n            string endpoint = DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            #region Snippet:ListConversations\\n            await foreach (CommunicationConversation conv in adminClient.GetConversationsAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Conversation id: {conv.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task SendConversationMessageAsync()\\n        {\\n            string endpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string conversationId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConversationThreadClient threadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(\\n                conversationId,\\n                new Uri(endpoint),\\n                new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            #region Snippet:SendConversationMessage\\n            SendConversationMessageOptions messageOptions = new SendConversationMessageOptions(\\n                new TextMessageContent(\\u0026#34;Hello from Azure Communication Messages!\\u0026#34;));\\n            Response sendResponse =\\n                await threadClient.SendMessageAsync(messageOptions);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Message id: {sendResponse.Value.MessageId}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task ListConversationMessagesAsync()\\n        {\\n            string endpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string conversationId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConversationThreadClient threadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(\\n                conversationId,\\n                new Uri(endpoint),\\n                new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            #region Snippet:ListConversationMessages\\n            await foreach (ConversationMessageItem message in threadClient.GetMessagesAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Message id: {message.Id}, sender: {message.SenderDisplayName}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task AddConversationParticipantAsync()\\n        {\\n            string endpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string conversationId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConversationThreadClient threadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(\\n                conversationId,\\n                new Uri(endpoint),\\n                new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            #region Snippet:AddConversationParticipant\\n            AddParticipantsOptions addOptions = new AddParticipantsOptions(\\n                new[] { new ConversationParticipant(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;) });\\n            await threadClient.AddParticipantsAsync(addOptions);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Adjust constructor signatures and model names to match the current generated API (check `api/Azure.Communication.Messages.net10.0.cs` for the authoritative shape). Use `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` around any test-only assertions or cleanup code.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` currently has all required top-level sections. The following are missing or incomplete:\\n\\n1. **Key concepts** — incomplete; missing `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`, `ConversationThreadClientSettings`\\n2. **Examples** — incomplete; missing all Conversation API usage examples\\n\\nThe sections to add/fix are listed in Step 1 above.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/src/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` — injects snippet regions into README.md. 2026-04-16T03:17:05.0549609Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T03:14:41.294761063Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58202,\"title\":\"[Communication Messages] Docs: README missing Conversation API documentation and examples\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #56911 (commit d2dee26) by `@MaiLinhP`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #56911 migrated `Azure.Communication.Messages` to the new TypeSpec emitter. The migration added `ConversationThreadClientSettings`, new `CollectionResults` for conversation pagination, and updated `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`. The package has offered Conversation APIs since `1.3.0-beta.1`, and these are now fully present in the public API surface (309 additions to the API listing files), but they remain entirely absent from the README.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` are not mentioned anywhere in `README.md` — the README only documents `NotificationMessagesClient` and `MessageTemplateClient`\\n- The **Key concepts** section lists only `NotificationMessagesClient`; `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`, and `ConversationThreadClientSettings` are not described\\n- No **Examples** section exists for Conversation APIs (create/get/delete conversations, send/list messages, add/remove participants, read receipts, typing indicators)\\n- Auto-generated sample files (`Samples_ConversationAdministrationClient.cs`, `Samples_ConversationThreadClient.cs`) exist in `tests/Samples/` but contain only compilation-only `[Ignore]` tests with no `#region Snippet:` regions — no human-readable snippets are wired into the README\\n- The CHANGELOG `1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section is completely empty despite the generator migration introducing `ConversationThreadClientSettings` and `CollectionResults` pagination types\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section to document the migration changes:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n- Added `ConversationThreadClientSettings` to support configuration-based setup of `ConversationThreadClient` via `IConfiguration`.\\n- Added strongly-typed collection result types (`ConversationAdministrationClientGetConversationsCollectionResult` and async variants) for paginating conversation lists.\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n- Migrated to the new TypeSpec-based code generator. Generated code is now located under `Generated/` at the package root.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md`\\n\\nEdit the README to add the Conversation API key concepts and examples. Changes needed:\\n\\n1. **Key concepts section** — replace or extend the current sparse paragraph:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Key concepts\\n\\n- `NotificationMessagesClient` provides the functionality to send notification messages (WhatsApp text, template, and media messages).\\n- `MessageTemplateClient` provides the functionality to list available WhatsApp message templates.\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` manages conversation lifecycle: create, retrieve, list, and delete conversations.\\n- `ConversationThreadClient` interacts with conversation threads: send messages, list messages, manage participants, send read receipts, and send typing indicators.\\n- `ConversationThreadClientSettings` enables DI/configuration-based setup of `ConversationThreadClient` when using `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration`.\\n```\\n\\n2. **Authenticate the client** subsection — add a snippet placeholder for `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` authentication:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Conversation clients\\n\\n`ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` support both connection string and token credential authentication.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n3. **Examples section** — add a new subsection for Conversation APIs after the existing notification examples:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Work with conversations\\n\\n#### Create a conversation\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### List conversations\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### Send a message in a conversation\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### List messages in a conversation\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### Add a participant to a conversation\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new human-readable sample file at:\\n**`sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationScenarios.cs`**\\n\\nThe auto-generated files (`Samples_ConversationAdministrationClient.cs`, `Samples_ConversationThreadClient.cs`) are `// ` and contain only compilation stubs with `[Ignore]`. Do not add snippet regions to those files. Create a new, hand-authored sample file instead.\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as a canonical example for the test file format.\\n\\nExample structure:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Communication.Messages.Models.Channels;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Communication.Messages.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class Sample_ConversationScenarios : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task CreateConversationAdministrationClientAsync()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateConversationAdministrationClient\\n            string endpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(\\n                new Uri(endpoint),\\n                new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task CreateConversationAsync()\\n        {\\n            string endpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(\\n                new Uri(endpoint),\\n                new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            #region Snippet:CreateConversation\\n            CommunicationConversation conversation = new CommunicationConversation();\\n            Response createResponse = await adminClient.CreateConversationAsync(conversation);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created conversation id: {createResponse.Value.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            // cleanup\\n            await adminClient.DeleteConversationAsync(createResponse.Value.Id);\\n#endif\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task ListConversationsAsync()\\n        {\\n            string endpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(\\n                new Uri(endpoint),\\n                new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            #region Snippet:ListConversations\\n            await foreach (CommunicationConversation conv in adminClient.GetConversationsAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Conversation id: {conv.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task SendConversationMessageAsync()\\n        {\\n            string endpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string conversationId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConversationThreadClient threadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(\\n                conversationId,\\n                new Uri(endpoint),\\n                new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            #region Snippet:SendConversationMessage\\n            SendConversationMessageOptions messageOptions = new SendConversationMessageOptions(\\n                new TextMessageContent(\\u0026#34;Hello from Azure Communication Messages!\\u0026#34;));\\n            Response sendResponse =\\n                await threadClient.SendMessageAsync(messageOptions);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Message id: {sendResponse.Value.MessageId}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task ListConversationMessagesAsync()\\n        {\\n            string endpoint = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string conversationId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConversationThreadClient threadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(\\n                conversationId,\\n                new Uri(endpoint),\\n                new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            #region Snippet:ListConversationMessages\\n            await foreach (ConversationMessageItem message in threadClient.GetMessagesAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Message id: {message.Id}, sender: {message.SenderDisplayName}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task AddConversationParticipantAsync()\\n        {\\n threadClient.AddParticipantsAsync(addOptions);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Adjust constructor signatures and model names to match the current generated API (check `api/Azure.Communication.Messages.net10.0.cs` for the authoritative shape). send notification messages (WhatsApp text, template, and media messages).\\n- `MessageTemplateClient` provides the functionality to list available WhatsApp message templates.\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` manages conversation lifecycle: create, retrieve, list, and delete conversations.\\n- `ConversationThreadClient` interacts with conversation threads: send messages, list messages, manage participants, send read receipts, and send typing indicators.\\n- `ConversationThreadClientSettings` enables DI/configuration-based setup of `ConversationThreadClient` when using `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration`.\\n```\\n\\n2. **Authenticate the client** subsection — add a snippet placeholder for `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`
